brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +57.3% since Feb 27 — Brent ~$111.68 (Apr 7 intraday); US strikes 50+ targets on Kharg Island; Israel launches wide-scale bridge/railway strikes; WTI spiking toward $115; 8 PM ET deadline approaching; ceasefire rejected
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 6 close: ~$111.25/bbl. Apr 7 intraday: $109.87–$111.68 range. US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports). Israel launched wide-scale bridge/railway strikes across Iran. WTI spiking toward $115/bbl on Kharg news. Trump's 8 PM ET deadline for Hormuz approaching — threatens 'Power Plant Day' and 'Bridge Day.' Iran rejected ceasefire but submitted 10-point counter-proposal. IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region.' Vance: 'military goals achieved' — possible off-ramp language. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / JPMorgan / IEA.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +72.9% since Feb 27 — WTI ~$115 (Apr 7 spiking on Kharg Island strikes); +2-3% intraday; Bloomberg: approaching $115-116; 8 PM ET deadline; IRGC threatens strikes 'outside the region'
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 6 close: ~$111.81/bbl. Apr 7 intraday: $112.42–$115.00 — spiking ~2-3% on Kharg Island strike reports. Bloomberg cites prices approaching $115-116/bbl. US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports). WTI-Brent spread remains inverted. Gas $4.119/gal nationally (AAA). Trump's 8 PM ET deadline approaching. IRGC issued most extreme threat yet: strikes 'outside the region.' Vance says 'military goals achieved' while Trump warns 'a whole civilisation will die tonight.' Source: NYMEX / AAA / Bloomberg / Goldman Sachs / JPMorgan / IEA / Rigzone.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.139 / +38.2% since Feb 27 — $4.104-$4.119 nationally (Apr 7 AAA) — CA $5.89 — OK $3.27 (lowest) — up $1.08 from one month ago — +27% since Mar 4
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Apr 7: $4.104-$4.119/gal nationally (AAA) — $1.139 surge in 39 days. Up from $3.20 on March 4 (+27.1%). Up $1.08/gal from one month ago. California $5.89/gal (highest). Oklahoma $3.27/gal (lowest). Diesel ~$5.43/gal nationally. Goldman: every $10/bbl crude increase adds ~$0.25/gal at pump. If WTI sustains above $115, pump prices could exceed $4.50 nationally. Source: AAA / EIA / Goldman Sachs.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.change: -267 pts / -3.9% — S&P 6,611.83 (Apr 6 close); Apr 7 opened ~6,588 under pressure; VIX 24.88 (+4.2%); Kharg strikes + deadline fear; Goldman warns 5,400 on persistent disruptions
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 6 close: 6,611.83 (+0.44%). Apr 7: opened ~6,587.66, range 6,579-6,618 — down ~0.5-0.7% on Kharg Island strikes and escalating rhetoric. S&P rose four consecutive sessions through Apr 6 (+3.3% for week) but reversing Apr 7. VIX: 24.17 (Apr 6) → pre-market 24.88 (+4.2%), range 23.78-25.30. Goldman Sachs: persistent oil disruptions could drag S&P to 5,400. Kalshi: 60% probability S&P drops below 5,900 in 2026. Gold $4,672-4,700/oz. 10Y yield 4.335%. Source: NYSE / Bloomberg / CNBC / Goldman Sachs / Kalshi / Moody's.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$1.0-1.5B/day — Kharg Island strikes (50+ targets) + Israeli bridge campaign escalate tempo — 82nd Airborne operational — 8 PM ET deadline imminent — B-52s flying over Iran with JDAMs
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 39: ~$1.0-1.5B/day (range $890M-$2B). US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island. Israel launched wide-scale bridge/railway strikes. B-52s flying over Iran with JDAM gravity bombs — signals degraded Iranian air defenses. 82nd Airborne 1st BCT operational in CENTCOM. Three CSGs: Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (sidelined after fire/repairs), Bush (Atlantic transit ETA Apr 10-12). 8 PM ET Hormuz deadline imminent. Pentagon $200B+ supplemental faces massive bipartisan resistance — 71% oppose. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / CENTCOM / USNI / Reuters / Stars and Stripes.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (39 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$46.9B through Day 39 — $200B supplemental: 71% oppose — only 34% approve military action (CNN) — 27% say war worth costs — Trump approval 30-35% on war — GOP support drops 83%→68%
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Pentagon: $11.3B through Day 6. CSIS: $16.5B through Day 12. Extrapolated through Day 39: ~$46.9B. Penn Wharton Budget Model: $40B-$95B in direct costs, up to $210B total economic impact. $200B supplemental: 71% oppose. CNN poll: only 34% approve military action (down 7 pts). YouGov: Trump war handling 30-33% approval. Fox News: 59% disapproval — highest either Trump term. Independents collapse 31%→22%. GOP drops 83%→68%. Only 27% say war worth costs (vs 59% for Iraq 2003). Source: Pentagon / CSIS / Penn Wharton / CNN / YouGov / Fox News / Congress.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CENTCOM: 13,000+ targets struck — 155 ships sunk/damaged — 26 aircraft types — B-52s now flying over Iran with JDAMs — Kharg Island 50+ targets Apr 7 — ~50% of missile launchers remain intact
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Stars and Stripes: 13,000+ targets struck, 155 Iranian ships sunk/damaged, 26 different aircraft types employed. Combined US/Israeli extrapolated ~35,500+ through Day 39. US struck 50+ military targets on Kharg Island (90% of Iran's oil exports) Apr 7. Israel launched wide-scale bridge/railway strikes. B-52s now flying over Iran with JDAM gravity bombs — signals significantly degraded air defenses. New capabilities: U-2 reconnaissance, EA-37B Compass Call, LUCAS drones. ~50% of Iran's missile launchers remain intact with thousands of OWADs still in arsenal. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / Stars and Stripes.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 15 US KIA confirmed — 140-520+ WIA (Pentagon vs Intercept) — F-15E pilot shot down and rescued — WSO rescued after 48+ hrs inside Iran — 7 aircraft lost + 12+ MQ-9 drones
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. 15 KIA confirmed as of Apr 1. Pentagon official figure ~140 wounded (Mar 10) disputed by The Intercept (520+ including smoke inhalation aboard USS Ford). F-15E WSO rescued Apr 5 after 48+ hours evading IRGC — CIA deception campaign + makeshift airbase inside Iran. Total aircraft losses: 7 airframes (3 F-15Es Kuwait friendly fire Mar 2; 1 KC-135 Mar 12; 1 F-15E shot down Apr 3; 1 A-10 Apr 3) + 12+ MQ-9 Reaper drones. Key incidents: 6 KIA Port Shuaiba Kuwait (Mar 1); 6 KIA KC-135 crash Iraq (Mar 12). Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Stars and Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: CENTCOM DMDC: 13 KIA, 365 WIA (Apr 3-4) — The Intercept: 15 KIA, 520+ WIA — accuses CENTCOM of 'casualty cover-up' — 15+ wounded at Prince Sultan AB Apr 4 may not be in CENTCOM count
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM Defense Manpower Data Center: 13 KIA, 365 WIA (as of Apr 3-4). The Intercept reports 15 KIA, 520+ WIA, accusing CENTCOM of 'casualty cover-up' by sending outdated figures. At least 15 troops wounded in Iranian attack on Prince Sultan Air Base Apr 4, which may not be reflected in older CENTCOM count. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / The Intercept / Reuters.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Health Min: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ injured — HRANA: 3,540-3,546+ (244+ children) — Iran Int'l: 4,700+ security forces — internet at 4% of normal — 307 health facilities damaged — 3.2M displaced
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — dramatic discrepancies across sources reflecting fog of war and internet blackout (connectivity at 4% of normal). Iran Health Ministry: 2,076+ killed, 26,500+ wounded. HRANA (NGO): 3,540-3,546+ (244 children killed, 15%+ of casualties under 18). IFRC/Red Crescent: 1,900 killed, 20,000 injured. Iran International: 4,700+ security forces (unverified). HRANA figure nearly double Health Ministry — includes military casualties government doesn't disclose. Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,000+. Two senior IRGC commanders killed Apr 6: Maj. Gen. Khademi (intelligence chief) + Asghar Bakeri (Quds Force Unit 840). 307 health/medical facilities damaged. 3.2M displaced. Source: Iran Health Ministry / HRANA / IFRC / Iran International / OCHA.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 1,497 killed — 4,639 wounded — 129 children — 53 medical workers — 1.2M+ displaced (~20% of population) — 150,000 cut off south of Litani — UNHCR Lebanon response only 14% funded
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli ground invasion since March 2. 1,497 killed. 4,639 wounded. 129 children killed. 53 medical workers killed. Over 1.2 million displaced (~20% of population). Up to 150,000 people cut off in southern Lebanon by destruction of Litani River bridges. UNHCR Lebanon response only 14% funded against $472M requirement. USAID largely dismantled. IDF permanent buffer zone south of Litani. 3 UNIFIL Indonesian peacekeepers killed. UNSC voted to terminate UNIFIL by end 2026. Source: Lebanon Health Ministry / UNICEF / UNHCR / Al Jazeera / IDF.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: UNHCR: 7.5M+ displaced — Iran 3.2M+ — Lebanon 1.2M+ (~20% of pop.) — 68,000+ Iranians into Turkey — 70,000+ Afghan refugees returned — 150,000 cut off south of Litani — UNHCR Lebanon only 14% funded
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. UNHCR: up to 3.2 million Iranians temporarily displaced — fleeing Tehran and major urban centers. Lebanon: over 1.2 million displaced (~20% of population), up to 150,000 cut off south of Litani by bridge destruction. Cross-border flows: 68,000+ Iranians into Turkey, 70,000+ Afghan refugees returned to Afghanistan (many forced), 30,000 crossing into Afghanistan from Iran. Total: ~7.5M+. UNHCR Lebanon response only 14% funded against $472M requirement. USAID largely dismantled. WHO $30.3M emergency appeal for health systems. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / UNICEF.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — Hormuz transits collapsed to ~12-20/day (85-91% below pre-war ~138/day) — Iran allowing select 'neutral' ships — earned ~$3B from ghost fleet tankers — 400+ vessels stranded
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Strait of Hormuz daily transits collapsed to ~12-20 vessels/day vs pre-conflict baseline ~138/day — 85-91% reduction. Iran allowing select 'neutral' country ships (China, Russia, India, Pakistan, etc.) and charging millions for transit. Iran earned estimated $3B from ~38M barrels shipped via ghost fleet tankers to China/Malaysia. 400+ vessels anchored in Gulf of Oman. 20,000+ seafarers stranded. War risk insurance removed for strait since Mar 5. Source: IMO / IATA / Lloyd's / Bloomberg / IEA.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: HRANA: 244+ children (Iran) + UNICEF: 129+ (Lebanon) + Israel 4 + Kuwait 1 — 6 children killed in Tehran overnight strikes Apr 6 — 15%+ of Iran casualties under 18 — 2 young protesters executed Apr 5
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. HRANA: 244+ children killed in Iran (15%+ of casualties under 18). UNICEF: 129+ children killed in Lebanon. Israel: 4. Kuwait: 1. Six children (4 girls, 2 boys under 10) killed in overnight strikes on Tehran residential area Apr 6. Two young protesters executed Apr 5 (ages 31 and 19) — regime continues executions under cover of war. Deadliest single incident: Minab girls' school (Feb 28) — 165-180 killed. Iran called for 'human chains' around power plants ahead of deadline — including children. Source: UNICEF / HRANA / Lebanese MoH / UNHRC / Amnesty International.
